changes: Expanded Meta type to include _thumbnail_id (number|null) and focal_point ({x,y}) fields

ImageDropUploader image prop now reads meta._thumbnail_id || featuredMediaId || null, matching the featured-image.tsx pattern where meta is the reliable source (thumbnail support is removed from the post type)
ImageDropUploader image prop passes focalPoint: meta.focal_point to respect any stored focal point

onSelect now writes both featured_media and meta._thumbnail_id: editPost({ featured_media: selected.id || null, meta: { ...meta, _thumbnail_id: selected.id || null } }) — identical pattern to featured-image.tsx

phpVerification.conclusion: Correct — no change needed to card-team-member.php
phpVerification.explanation: _thumbnail_id is WordPress's own internal postmeta key for the post thumbnail. WordPress's get_post_thumbnail_id() reads this key directly from the postmeta table via get_post_meta(). Registering it as a REST-exposed meta field (in inc/functions/featured-image.php) only adds REST API visibility; it does not change how WordPress reads or writes the value at the database level. The card template already uses get_post_thumbnail_id() which will correctly return the value written by either the sidebar FeaturedImagePanel or the block body ImageDropUploader.

patternAlignment.imageIdRead: meta._thumbnail_id || featuredMediaId (block) vs postThumbnail || meta._thumbnail_id (panel) — both cover the same fallback cases
patternAlignment.onSelectWrite: editPost({ featured_media, meta: { ...meta, _thumbnail_id } }) — identical in both locations
patternAlignment.focalPoint: Block body passes focalPoint to ImageDropUploader for display; panel writes focal_point to meta on select. The block body ImageDropUploader does not write focal_point on select to avoid overwriting a value set via the sidebar panel.

fixesApplied: Align ImageDropUploader image source with meta._thumbnail_id (the authoritative source when thumbnail support is removed)
Match featured-image.tsx dual-write pattern on select: featured_media + meta._thumbnail_id
